_meta.name: city_registry_extended
_meta.resolved_by: P2 recon (live curl probes against each Socrata host). Companion to city_registry.resolved.json; does NOT modify it.
_meta.description: SUPPLEMENTARY, display-only Socrata feeds that expand each city's KPI list beyond the 3-pillar backbone (food inspections, traffic crashes, towed vehicles, business licenses, etc.). These are NOT folded into the City Pulse composite (every feed carries supplementary:true). They flow through the EXISTING socrata adapter (monthly_counts / feed_series) unchanged. Chicago is the template (4 feeds). Miami (ArcGIS) is out of scope for this pass.
_meta.source_of_truth: CITY_TAB_BUILD.md build sequence P2 ('Expand per-city KPI lists beyond the backbone ... Chicago is the template'); per-feed scoring shape in section 2.

conventions.feed_keys: Same keys as a resolved-registry Socrata feed: label, dataset, date_col, date_col_status, polarity, metric, note. Plus supplementary:true and pillar_hint.
conventions.supplementary: Always true here. Display-only; excluded from the Pulse composite (C) computation.
conventions.pillar_hint: Which backbone pillar this KPI relates to for display grouping: public_safety | development_economy | city_services | context. 'context' = no clean pillar fit / polarity 0.
conventions.polarity: +1 up-is-favorable, -1 down-is-favorable, 0 context-only (raw counts that aren't clearly good/bad).

notes_global: All date_cols confirmed live 2026-05-31 via curl probe (limit=1 for the id+column, plus a date_trunc_ym monthly aggregation to confirm recent data). May 2026 is the current (incomplete) month everywhere; latest COMPLETE month = 2026-04, matching city_registry.resolved.json as_of_complete_month.

Same Socrata throttling rules as the backbone: pass SOCRATA_APP_TOKEN ($$app_token query or X-App-Token header) to avoid keyless 429s on the larger tables (Chicago crashes ~900k+, NYC inspections, NYC collisions).

Polarity 0 feeds (inspection/response-call/tow VOLUME) are context-only by design: raw counts conflate workload/enforcement intensity with outcomes. The signal in inspections is the pass/fail RESULT, not how many were performed; do not score these into a pillar.
Miami (Miami-Dade, ArcGIS Hub) intentionally skipped this pass; see CITY_TAB_BUILD.md section 3 watch-items.

chicago.label: Food Inspections
chicago.note: CONFIRMED live (max inspection_date 2026-05-29; ~1.4k/mo). Polarity 0: inspection VOLUME is workload, not an outcome — more or fewer inspections is neither good nor bad on its own (the editorial signal is the Fail/Pass rate via the `results` column, not count). pillar_hint context. Latest complete month 2026-04.

chicago.label: Traffic Crashes
chicago.note: CONFIRMED live (max crash_date 2026-05-30; ~8-9k/mo, dense). Polarity -1: fewer reported crashes is favorable (Vision-Zero framing). pillar_hint public_safety but SUPPLEMENTARY — display alongside the crime pillar, not summed into it. Latest complete month 2026-04.

chicago.label: Towed Vehicles
chicago.note: CONFIRMED live (max tow_date 2026-05-31) BUT this is a ROLLING ~90-day inventory snapshot, not a historical series: min(tow_date)=2026-03-03, max=2026-05-31, total ~4.7k rows. CANNOT support a 12-month baseline or YoY — render as a recent-volume tile only (status insufficient_history in scorer output); do NOT z-score. Polarity 0: tow counts reflect enforcement/relocation activity, not safety or wellbeing. pillar_hint context.

chicago.label: Business Licenses
chicago.note: CONFIRMED live. license_start_date gives a clean recent flow (~2.0-2.3k/mo) ONCE future-dated rows are filtered: raw agg returns a large NULL bucket (~2.7k) plus forward-dated noise (2026-08/09/11), so filter `license_start_date IS NOT NULL AND license_start_date < <next-month>` before bucketing. date_issued is an even cleaner alternative (~2.3-2.7k/mo, no future noise, max 2026-05-29) and mirrors the backbone Building-Permits issue_date framing — use it if the future-date filter on license_start_date proves fragile. Polarity +1: new/renewed licenses up-is-favorable. pillar_hint development_economy. Latest complete month 2026-04.

nyc.label: DOHMH Restaurant Inspections
nyc.note: CONFIRMED live (~6.5-9.3k/mo, dense). MUST filter placeholder rows: ~3,434 records carry inspection_date='1900-01-01' (never-inspected / new establishments) — filter `inspection_date > '1901-01-01'` before bucketing or date_trunc_ym buries a huge 1900 bucket. Polarity 0: inspection VOLUME is workload, not outcome (signal = critical_flag / score, not count). pillar_hint context. Latest complete month 2026-04.

nyc.label: Motor Vehicle Collisions - Crashes
nyc.note: CONFIRMED live (max crash_date 2026-05-26; ~6-6.7k/mo, dense). NYC Vision Zero crash file. Polarity -1: fewer collisions is favorable. pillar_hint public_safety, SUPPLEMENTARY (display alongside the NYPD-complaints pillar, not summed in). Latest complete month 2026-04.

sf.label: Registered Business Locations
sf.note: CONFIRMED live (data_as_of 2026-05-30; max dba_start_date 2026-05-29). dba_start_date gives a clean dense flow of new business registrations (~0.75-1.5k/mo) when filtered to a recent window. location_start_date is the alternative (original location open date; slightly more back-loaded). Polarity +1: new business registrations up-is-favorable. pillar_hint development_economy. Latest complete month 2026-04.

seattle.label: Fire 911 Calls
seattle.note: CONFIRMED live (max datetime 2026-05-30 23:51; very dense ~8.2-9.4k/mo). Seattle Fire Dept real-time 911 dispatch log. Polarity 0: fire/medic call VOLUME is demand/workload, not a safety outcome (rises with population, weather, season; no clean good/bad direction). pillar_hint context — render as a city-services activity tile, do not z-score into a pillar. Latest complete month 2026-04.
